requirements_addressed: Tests use pytest.raises(ValidationError) for negative cases

completion_promises.evidence: TestBDDBoundaryTemperature class contains 4 direct boundary tests (0.0 accepted, 2.0 accepted, 2.1 rejected, -0.1 rejected) plus parametrized tests covering 8 in-range values and 7 out-of-range values. All 19 boundary temperature tests pass.

Three negative test classes: TestBDDNegativeProvider (missing provider, invalid providers azure/huggingface/google, None, empty string, integer type), TestBDDNegativeEndpoint (local without endpoint, empty endpoint, malformed URL, parametrized non-HTTP schemes ftp/ws/no-scheme/missing-scheme/http-no-host), TestBDDNegativeModel (missing model, empty model, None model). All 16 negative validation tests pass.

Four edge case test classes: TestBDDEdgeCaseDefaults (default temperature 0.7, default empty endpoint), TestBDDEdgeCaseCloudProviders (anthropic/openai without endpoint accepted, anthropic/openai with custom endpoint accepted), TestBDDEdgeCaseFullConfig (full valid config with all fields, local with HTTPS endpoint), TestBDDEdgeCaseHeterogeneousConfig (different providers, different temperatures). All 12 edge case tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tests use pytest.raises(ValidationError) for negative cases
completion_promises.evidence: All negative and boundary-rejection tests consistently use `with pytest.raises(ValidationError)` pattern, with `match='endpoint'` for endpoint-related error messages. Follows the same pattern as synthesis/tests/test_validator.py.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tests are in tests/test_model_config.py or config/tests/test_models.py
completion_promises.evidence: All BDD tests are added to the existing config/tests/test_models.py file, extending the TASK-AF-001 test suite. This matches one of the two accepted locations specified in the acceptance criteria.
